message.content:
{
  "assessment": {
    "assessmentMeta": {
      "assessmentType": "Session assessment",
      "totalMarks": 20,
      "durationMinutes": 15,
      "preferredDifficulty": "Balanced",
      "language": "English",
      "instructions": [
        "Answer all questions based only on the concepts taught in Session 1 of Tissues.",
        "Read each question carefully before answering.",
        "Ensure your answers are clear and concise."
      ]
    },
    "blueprint": {
      "learningOutcomeCoverage": [],
      "difficultyDistribution": {
        "easy": 40,
        "medium": 35,
        "hard": 25
      },
      "bloomsDistribution": {
        "recall": 30,
        "understanding": 60,
        "application": 10,
        "analysis": 0,
        "evaluation": 0
      },
      "questionDistribution": {
        "mcq": 5,
        "shortAnswer": 4,
        "longAnswer": 1
      },
      "timeAllocation": [
        {
          "section": "MCQ",
          "minutes": 3
        },
        {
          "section": "Very Short Answer & Short Answer",
          "minutes": 7
        },
        {
          "section": "Long Answer",
          "minutes": 5
        }
      ]
    },
    "mcq": [
      {
        "id": "MCQ-1",
        "question": "Which of the following best defines a tissue?",
        "options": [
          "A. A group of cells with similar structure and function working together.",
          "B. An organ made up of different types of tissues.",
          "C. A single cell performing all life functions.",
          "D. A collection of organs forming an organism."
        ],
        "answer": "A",
        "explanation": "A tissue is defined as a group or layer of similar cells that work together to perform a specific function.",
        "marks": 1,
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Definition of Tissue"
        ],
        "difficulty": "easy",
        "bloomsLevel": "recall"
      },
      {
        "id": "MCQ-2",
        "question": "In plant tissues, which type is primarily responsible for providing mechanical strength and flexibility?",
        "options": [
          "A. Parenchyma",
          "B. Collenchyma",
          "C. Xylem vessels only",
          "D. Phloem"
        ],
        "answer": "B",
        "explanation": "Collenchyma tissues provide mechanical support and flexibility to growing parts of the plant.",
        "marks": 1,
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Mechanical Support"
        ],
        "difficulty": "easy",
        "bloomsLevel": "understanding"
      },
      {
        "id": "MCQ-3",
        "question": "Which of the following is a characteristic feature of parenchyma cells?",
        "options": [
          "A. Thickened cell walls at corners.",
          "B. Dead cells with thick lignified walls.",
          "C. Living cells with thin cellulose walls and large intercellular spaces.",
          "D. Elongated cells forming the vascular bundles."
        ],
        "answer": "C",
        "explanation": "Parenchyma consists of living, relatively unspecialized cells that often have thin cell walls and air spaces between them for storage or gas exchange.",
        "marks": 1,
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Parenchyma"
        ],
        "difficulty": "easy",
        "bloomsLevel": "recall"
      },
      {
        "id": "MCQ-4",
        "question": "Sclerenchymatous cells are typically dead at maturity. What is the primary reason for this?",
        "options": [
          "A. They lose their protoplasm to become flexible.",
          "B. Their cell walls get thickened with lignin, making them rigid and non-living.",
          "C. They require energy only during growth phases.",
          "D. They are designed for photosynthesis which stops at maturity."
        ],
        "answer": "B",
        "explanation": "Sclerenchyma cells become dead because their cell walls undergo extensive thickening due to the deposition of lignin, providing hardness and rigidity.",
        "marks": 1,
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Sclerenchyma"
        ],
        "difficulty": "medium",
        "bloomsLevel": "understanding"
      },
      {
        "id": "MCQ-5",
        "question": "Which tissue type is found in the husk of a coconut and provides hardness?",
        "options": [
          "A. Parenchyma",
          "B. Collenchyma",
          "C. Sclerenchyma",
          "D. Epidermis"
        ],
        "answer": "C",
        "explanation": "The husk of a coconut is hard due to the presence of sclerenchymatous fibers, which are dead and lignified.",
        "marks": 1,
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Sclerenchyma"
        ],
        "difficulty": "medium",
        "bloomsLevel": "application"
      }
    ],
    "shortAnswer": [
      {
        "id": "SA-1",
        "question": "Name the two main types of plant tissues based on cell division capability.",
        "answer": "Meristematic tissue and Permanent tissue.",
        "expectedLength": "2 terms listed clearly",
        "marks": 2,
        "rubric": [
          "1 mark for correctly naming 'Meristematic tissue'",
          "1 mark for correctly naming 'Permanent tissue'"
        ],
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Classification of Plant Tissues"
        ],
        "difficulty": "easy",
        "bloomsLevel": "recall"
      },
      {
        "id": "SA-2",
        "question": "Differentiate between Parenchyma and Sclerenchyma based on the nature of their cell walls.",
        "answer": "Parenchyma cells have thin, flexible cellulose walls. Sclerenchyma cells have thick, rigid lignified walls.",
        "expectedLength": "1-2 clear points",
        "marks": 2,
        "rubric": [
          "1 mark for describing Parenchyma cell wall (thin/cellulose)",
          "1 mark for describing Sclerenchyma cell wall (thick/lignified)"
        ],
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Comparison"
        ],
        "difficulty": "medium",
        "bloomsLevel": "understanding"
      },
      {
        "id": "SA-3",
        "question": "List two functions of the epidermis in plants.",
        "answer": "1. Protects inner tissues from water loss and injury.\n2. Prevents entry of harmful microorganisms or gases exchange (via stomata).",
        "expectedLength": "2 clear points",
        "marks": 3,
        "rubric": [
          "1 mark for protection against physical damage/water loss.",
          "1 mark for preventing pathogen entry/gas regulation via stomata.",
          "1 mark for clarity and correct terminology."
        ],
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Epidermis"
        ],
        "difficulty": "medium",
        "bloomsLevel": "understanding"
      },
      {
        "id": "SA-4",
        "question": "Why are intercellular spaces absent in Sclerenchyma tissue?",
        "answer": "Intercellular spaces are absent because the cells are tightly packed with thick, lignified walls to provide maximum mechanical strength and rigidity.",
        "expectedLength": "1-2 clear points",
        "marks": 3,
        "rubric": [
          "1 mark for mentioning tight packing or lack of space.",
          "1 mark for linking this structure to the function (strength/rigidity).",
          "1 mark for correct reasoning."
        ],
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Sclerenchyma Structure"
        ],
        "difficulty": "hard",
        "bloomsLevel": "understanding"
      }
    ],
    "longAnswer": [
      {
        "id": "LA-1",
        "question": "Describe the structure and function of Collenchyma tissue. How does it differ from Sclerenchyma?",
        "answer": "Collenchyma consists of living cells with unevenly thickened cell walls (rich in pectin) at corners, providing flexibility and mechanical support to growing parts like stems and leaf veins.\n\nDifferences:\n1. Cell Status: Collenchyma is living; Sclerenchyma is dead at maturity.\n2. Wall Composition: Collenchyma has cellulose/pectin thickening (flexible); Sclerenchyma has lignified walls (rigid).\n3. Function: Collenchyma provides flexibility for bending without breaking; Sclerenchyma provides hardness and rigidity.",
        "expectedLength": "4-5 well-structured points with explanation",
        "marks": 5,
        "rubric": [
          "1 mark for defining structure (living cells, uneven thickening).",
          "1 mark for stating function (flexibility/support in growing parts).",
          "1 mark for correctly identifying Collenchyma as living.",
          "1 mark for correct comparison point regarding cell wall composition or rigidity vs flexibility.",
          "1 mark for clear structure and logical flow."
        ],
        "learningOutcomeIds": [],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Plant Tissues - Collenchyma",
          "Comparison of Plant Tissues"
        ],
        "difficulty": "hard",
        "bloomsLevel": "analysis"
      }
    ],
    "answerKey": {
      "mcq": [
        {
          "answer": "A. A group of cells with similar structure and function working together.",
          "explanation": "Definition of tissue.",
          "marks": 1
        },
        {
          "answer": "B. Collenchyma",
          "explanation": "Provides mechanical strength/flexibility.",
          "marks": 1
        },
        {
          "answer": "C. Living cells with thin cellulose walls and large intercellular spaces.",
          "explanation": "Characteristics of parenchyma.",
          "marks": 1
        },
        {
          "answer": "B. Their cell walls get thickened with lignin, making them rigid and non-living.",
          "explanation": "Reason for sclerenchyma cells being dead.",
          "marks": 1
        },
        {
          "answer": "C. Sclerenchyma",
          "explanation": "Found in coconut husk due to lignin content.",
          "marks": 1
        }
      ],
      "shortAnswer": [
        {
          "answer": "Meristematic tissue and Permanent tissue.",
          "rubric": [
            "1 mark for 'Meristematic'",
            "1 mark for 'Permanent'"
          ],
          "marks": 2
        },
        {
          "answer": "Parenchyma: Thin, flexible cellulose walls. Sclerenchyma: Thick, rigid lignified walls.",
          "rubric": [
            "1 mark for Parenchyma description",
            "1 mark for Sclerenchyma description"
          ],
          "marks": 2
        },
        {
          "answer": "Protection from water loss and injury; prevents entry of pathogens.",
          "rubric": [
            "1 mark for protection function",
            "1 mark for pathogen/gas exchange role",
            "1 mark for clarity"
          ],
          "marks": 3
        },
        {
          "answer": "Cells are tightly packed with thick lignified walls to maximize strength and rigidity.",
          "rubric": [
            "1 mark for tight packing/lack of space",
            "1 mark for link to mechanical strength/rigidity",
            "1 mark for correct reasoning"
          ],
          "marks": 3
        }
      ],
      "longAnswer": [
        {
          "answer": "Structure: Living cells, unevenly thickened walls (corners), flexible. Function: Support in growing parts.\nDifferences:\n1. Collenchyma is living; Sclerenchyma is dead.\n2. Walls are cellulose/pectin vs lignified.",
          "rubric": [
            "Structure description",
            "Function description (flexibility)",
            "Living status distinction",
            "Wall composition difference",
            "Logical flow/clarity"
          ],
          "marks": 5
        }
      ],
      "generalMarkingGuidance": [
        "Award marks point-wise for each valid idea.",
        "Accept equivalent scientific wording if the meaning is correct (e.g., 'thickened' vs 'lignified' where context allows).",
        "For long answers, reward structure and clear differentiation between concepts."
      ]
    }
  }
}
